_note: Stage-3 audit router: detect a test's stack → route to the tool that can prove it, with a provenance label, never a gate. Rubric from fixtures/audit-orchestrator/expected-findings.md, Case A (app-driven Playwright, epic-stack search.test.ts — a warm sibling, NOT vendored). The flagship judgment is the REACHABILITY WALL: source-mutating tools (StrykerJS/Tautest) can't reach app-driven code, so the target routes to /audit-test (dev-served, reachability guard); recommending Stryker/Tautest here is the signature failure — they'd survive every mutation → a false 🔴. Each must_surface is one groundable proposition (the compound-claim lesson from #81/#84). `anchors` exist only for the offline heuristic judge; the LLM judge grades `claim` directly. Live path needs the epic-stack sibling; offline --self-test grades the samples.

must_surface.claim: Detects the stack as app-driven Playwright — signalled by `playwright.config.ts` and the target's location under `tests/e2e/`, not an import sniff (epic-stack specs import a wrapper, not `@playwright/test`).
Routes to `/audit-test` (dev-served, reachability guard) because source-mutating tools can't reach app-driven code — the reachability wall.
Preserves audit-test's honest 🟡 on a stale/build-served harness instead of a false 🔴 — the reachability guard, and labels the routing Confirmed.

must_not.claim: Must NOT recommend StrykerJS or Tautest for this app-driven Playwright target — source-mutating tools can't reach app-driven code and would survive every mutation, yielding a false 🔴 (the reachability wall). Naming them to explain the wall is fine; routing to / telling the user to run them is not.
Must NOT emit a PASS/FAIL ship gate — it routes and carries a credibility read; the ship verdict is `/sentinel` → Gate.
